title: Crime and law
explanation:
Crime and law

  • crime words: theft, robbery, suspect, witness, evidence
  • legal words: judge, lawyer, court, prison, fine
  • verbs: steal, arrest, accuse, sentence

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 1
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: The police found enough ____ to continue the investigation. | *evidence | headline | vacancy
A ____ saw the accident and called emergency services. | *witness | judge | suspect
The shop was closed after a violent ____. | *robbery | promotion | documentary
The officer decided to ____ the man at the scene. | *arrest | broadcast | resign

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option. Use each option ONLY ONCE.

questions.text: The ____ listened to both sides before making a decision. | judge
He had to pay a large ____ for parking illegally. | fine
A defence ____ represented the accused man. | lawyer
The police questioned the main ____ all afternoon. | suspect
She was found guilty in ____. | court

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 3
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: He went to ____ for six months. | *prison | court | evidence
questions.feedback: Prison is where criminals are kept.

questions.text: Bike ____ has increased in the area recently. | *theft | witness | sentence
The judge will ____ the defendant tomorrow. | *sentence | steal | delay
She was accused of a serious ____.  | *crime | contract | routine
